title: Double the Marduk Double the Fun
display_title: Double the Marduk Double the Fun
wikitext:
[]: Double the Marduk, Double the Fun! [Spoilers]             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:47 am At the beginning of 2.0 , Kaji says "Bethania Base's Marduk Project is at an impasse." Did they forget the term "Marduk" was already used in 1.0 to reprise its original role from NGE, or does the American NERV branch somehow have a role in selecting the Eva pilots?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Sep 09, 2009 10:40 am It probably has something to do with the fact that Gendo said that the Evas past Unit 04 (I think) shouldn't have been built. Just like he doesn't have control over the construction, he doesn't get to use his front to pick who uses those units, because Seele has its own puppets in charge of that.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 09, 2009 1:36 pm Apparently there are enough Evangelions to require the Vatican Treaty. The Marduk Institute thus has branches all over the world.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Fri Sep 11, 2009 12:56 am Yet in the end, Kaji revealed to Misato that there is no Marduk Institute. NERV is the one pulling the strings.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Sep 11, 2009 8:48 am trueno_twentyone wrote: Yet in the end of Neon Genesis Evangelion , Kaji revealed to Misato that there is no Marduk Institute. NERV is the one pulling the strings.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Sep 11, 2009 11:26 am there was no marduk, but there were dummy companies that led people to believe marduk did exist. it makes sense that people would also believe that this institute, much like NERV, extends to several branches globally. it's not a stretch that they could be trying to find pilots overseas, right? or maybe this "marduk project" thing is something completely different and unrelated. we don't really know, and Kaji's baaaaad engrish doesn't help.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 11, 2009 1:28 pm The big twist of Rebuild is that the Marduk Institute is 100% real, and is a morally good organization with no secret, hidden plots beyond saving the world from the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Sep 11, 2009 4:04 pm ...marduk... real...? OMGSHOCKHORROR. (actually, that would be quite a good plot twist). hey, maybe mari actually works for marduk. and that's how she knew puppy-kun was a pilot. lol.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 11, 2009 5:26 pm carla wrote: ...marduk... real...? OMGSHOCKHORROR. (actually, that would be quite a good plot twist). hey, maybe mari actually works for marduk. and that's how she knew puppy-kun was a pilot. lol.

[]: Re: Double the Marduk, Double the Fun! [Spoilers]             Quote Post by EdZ » Fri Sep 11, 2009 9:49 pm NAveryW wrote: At the beginning of 2.0 , Kaji says "Bethania Base's Marduk Project is at an impasse." Did they forget the term "Marduk" was already used in 1.0 to reprise its original role from NGE, or does the American NERV branch somehow have a role in selecting the Eva pilots?

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Fri Sep 11, 2009 10:03 pm For Seele/Nerv, a giant synthetic robot that goes on a berserk rage whenever it feels like and you have no control or prediction of when is sort of a problem.....They take what they can get. As long as everybody becomes orange juice in the end.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun Sep 13, 2009 12:19 am Ok, given that Marduk is real, does it still serve it's purpose, and how come did Misato said in 1.0 that Gendou forced Shinji to pilot Unit 01? Shouldn't she have said that it's strange why the Marduk Institute selected the commander's son.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Sep 13, 2009 7:11 am Maybe 'Marduk Project' refers to SEELE's efforts to control any and all things to do with the EVA and Angels, with the Marduk Institute being the primary method of keeping tabs on everything ...or something

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun Sep 13, 2009 8:33 am According to the wiki, Marduk is an organization which is responsible of selecting pilots for Evangelions. My two cents about the Marduk project in Bethany base is that when Provisional Unit 05 was destroyed, thanks to Mari, it also means that there will be no need of a pilot in Bethany because there will be nothing to pilot.
